comment.body: I forgot the Username of the guy who originally built the script, that automatically splits Alma's inputs and shows the right levels on every chart. However, the script stopped working yesterday, because of changes in how alma posted the reversion levels. So I updated the script. Credit is to the original author, this was a minor thing to do and it was easy because the script is well written. :-)
comment.raw_body: I forgot the Username of the guy who originally built the script, that automatically splits Alma's inputs and shows the right levels on every chart. However, the script stopped working yesterday, because of changes in how alma posted the reversion levels. So I updated the script. Credit is to the original author, this was a minor thing to do and it was easy because the script is well written. :-)

comment.body:
//@version=6
indicator('Mean Reversion Threshold Levels', overlay = true)


// Input for multiple symbols with their respective threshold levels
input_text = input.text_area('', title = 'Input Symbol Thresholds (Format: SYMBOL on one line, thresholds on next line)', confirm = true)


// Get current symbol
current_symbol = syminfo.ticker


// Color inputs for different levels
color0 = input.color(defval = color.rgb(224, 10, 110), title = 'Color 1')
color1 = input.color(defval = color.rgb(224, 10, 110), title = 'Color 2')
color2 = input.color(defval = color.rgb(224, 10, 110), title = 'Color 3')
color3 = input.color(defval = color.rgb(224, 10, 110), title = 'Color 4')
color4 = input.color(defval = color.rgb(224, 10, 110), title = 'Color 5')
color5 = input.color(defval = color.rgb(224, 10, 110), title = 'Color 6')
color6 = input.color(defval = color.rgb(224, 10, 110), title = 'Color 7')
color7 = input.color(defval = color.rgb(224, 10, 110), title = 'Color 8')
color8 = input.color(defval = color.rgb(224, 10, 110), title = 'Color 9')
color9 = input.color(defval = color.rgb(224, 10, 110), title = 'Color 10')


// Function to split string by a delimiter
TxtToArray(string origin, string divider) =>
    str.split(origin, divider)


// Updated parsing logic supporting old and new formats
ParseSymbolThresholds(string inputText) =>
    symbolThresholds = array.new_string()
    if str.length(inputText) > 0
        lines = TxtToArray(inputText, '\n')
        currentSymbol = ''
        for i = 0 to array.size(lines) - 1
            line = str.trim(array.get(lines, i))


            // New format: === SYMBOL ===
            if str.startswith(line, '===') and str.endswith(line, '===') and str.length(line) > 7
                words = TxtToArray(line," ")
                symbolName = array.get(words,1)
                currentSymbol := symbolName
            // Old format: line without commas and not empty
            else if not str.contains(line, ',') and str.length(line) > 0
                currentSymbol := str.trim(line)
            // If this line is data and currentSymbol is set
            else if str.contains(line, ',') and str.length(currentSymbol) > 0
                array.push(symbolThresholds, currentSymbol)
                array.push(symbolThresholds, line)
                currentSymbol := ''
    symbolThresholds


// Normalize and match against current symbol
GetCurrentSymbolThresholds(array<string> symbolThresholds, string currentSymbol) =>
    result = ''
    normalizedCurrent = str.upper(currentSymbol)
    for i = 0 to array.size(symbolThresholds) - 1 by 2
        if i + 1 < array.size(symbolThresholds)
            symbol = array.get(symbolThresholds, i)
            thresholds = array.get(symbolThresholds, i + 1)


            normalizedInput = str.upper(symbol)
            normalizedInput := str.replace(normalizedInput, '=F', '1!')
            normalizedInput := normalizedInput == 'ES' ? 'ES1!' : normalizedInput


            if normalizedInput == normalizedCurrent
                result := thresholds
                break
    result


// Parse input and get current symbol thresholds
symbolThresholds = ParseSymbolThresholds(input_text)
currentThresholds = GetCurrentSymbolThresholds(symbolThresholds, current_symbol)
Dividedstrings = TxtToArray(currentThresholds, ',')


// Plot levels
plot(array.size(Dividedstrings) >= 2 ? str.tonumber(array.get(Dividedstrings, 1)) : na, title = 'level 1', color = color0, display = display.price_scale)
plot(array.size(Dividedstrings) >= 4 ? str.tonumber(array.get(Dividedstrings, 3)) : na, title = 'level 2', color = color1, display = display.price_scale)
plot(array.size(Dividedstrings) >= 6 ? str.tonumber(array.get(Dividedstrings, 5)) : na, title = 'level 3', color = color2, display = display.price_scale)
plot(array.size(Dividedstrings) >= 8 ? str.tonumber(array.get(Dividedstrings, 7)) : na, title = 'level 4', color = color3, display = display.price_scale)
plot(array.size(Dividedstrings) >= 10 ? str.tonumber(array.get(Dividedstrings, 9)) : na, title = 'level 5', color = color4, display = display.price_scale)
plot(array.size(Dividedstrings) >= 12 ? str.tonumber(array.get(Dividedstrings, 11)) : na, title = 'level 6', color = color5, display = display.price_scale)
plot(array.size(Dividedstrings) >= 14 ? str.tonumber(array.get(Dividedstrings, 13)) : na, title = 'level 7', color = color6, display = display.price_scale)
plot(array.size(Dividedstrings) >= 16 ? str.tonumber(array.get(Dividedstrings, 15)) : na, title = 'level 8', color = color7, display = display.price_scale)
plot(array.size(Dividedstrings) >= 18 ? str.tonumber(array.get(Dividedstrings, 17)) : na, title = 'level 9', color = color8, display = display.price_scale)
plot(array.size(Dividedstrings) >= 20 ? str.tonumber(array.get(Dividedstrings, 19)) : na, title = 'level 10', color = color9, display = display.price_scale)


// Add labels and lines
if barstate.islastconfirmedhistory and array.size(Dividedstrings) != 0
    for i = 0 to array.size(Dividedstrings) - 1 by 1
        temporarycolor = i <= 1 ? color0 :
                         i <= 3 ? color1 :
                         i <= 5 ? color2 :
                         i <= 7 ? color3 :
                         i <= 9 ? color4 :
                         i <= 11 ? color5 :
                         i <= 13 ? color6 :
                         i <= 15 ? color7 :
                         i <= 17 ? color8 :
                         color9


        if i % 2 == 0 and array.size(Dividedstrings) > i + 1
            label.new(bar_index, str.tonumber(array.get(Dividedstrings, i + 1)), array.get(Dividedstrings, i),
                      xloc = xloc.bar_index, color = color.new(color.white, 10),
                      size = size.large, style = label.style_none,
                      textcolor = temporarycolor, textalign = text.align_right)


        if i % 2 != 0 and array.size(Dividedstrings) > i
            line.new(bar_index, str.tonumber(array.get(Dividedstrings, i)),
                     bar_index + 1, str.tonumber(array.get(Dividedstrings, i)),
                     extend = extend.both, color = temporarycolor)




//@version=6



tickers = array.from("ES1!","SPY","SPX")
count = array.size(tickers)
times = array.new<int>()
prices = array.new<float>()



var table price_tab = table.new(position.top_center,3,count,color.white)


for i = 0 to (count - 1)
    t = array.get(tickers,i)
    close_t = request.security(t,"",time_close)
    times.push(close_t)


close_time = times.min()


for i = 0 to (count - 1)
    t = array.get(tickers,i)
    sec = request.security(t,"",close)
    prices.push(sec)



for i = 0 to (count - 1)
    t = array.get(tickers,i)
    p_int = array.get(prices,i)
    p = str.tostring(p_int)
    s = str.tostring(array.get(prices,0) - p_int)
    price_tab.cell(0,i,t)
    price_tab.cell(1,i,p)
    price_tab.cell(2,i,s)

indicator('Mean Reversion Threshold Levels', overlay = true)


// Input for multiple symbols with their respective threshold levels
input_text = input.text_area('', title = 'Input Symbol Thresholds (Format: SYMBOL on one line, thresholds on next line)', confirm = true)


// Get current symbol
current_symbol = syminfo.ticker


// Color inputs for different levels
color0 = input.color(defval = color.rgb(224, 10, 110), title = 'Color 1')
color1 = input.color(defval = color.rgb(224, 10, 110), title = 'Color 2')

// Function to split string by a delimiter
TxtToArray(string origin, string divider) =>
    str.split(origin, divider)


// Updated parsing logic supporting old and new formats
ParseSymbolThresholds(string inputText) =>

                words = TxtToArray(line," ")

                currentSymbol := symbolName
            // Old format: line without commas and not empty
            else if not str.contains(line, ',') and str.length(line) > 0
                currentSymbol := str.trim(line)

            else if str.contains(line, ',') and str.length(currentSymbol) > 0
                array.push(symbolThresholds, currentSymbol)
                array.push(symbolThresholds, line)
                currentSymbol := ''
    symbolThresholds


// Normalize and match against current symbol
GetCurrentSymbolThresholds(array<string> symbolThresholds, string currentSymbol) =>
    result = ''

            thresholds = array.get(symbolThresholds, i + 1)


            normalizedInput = str.upper(symbol)
            normalizedInput := str.replace(normalizedInput, '=F', '1!')
            normalizedInput := normalizedInput == 'ES' ? 'ES1!' : normalizedInput


            if normalizedInput == normalizedCurrent
                result := thresholds
                break
    result


// Parse input and get current symbol thresholds
symbolThresholds = ParseSymbolThresholds(input_text)
currentThresholds = GetCurrentSymbolThresholds(symbolThresholds, current_symbol)
Dividedstrings = TxtToArray(currentThresholds, ',')


// Plot levels
plot(array.size(Dividedstrings) >= 2 ? str.tonumber(array.get(Dividedstrings, 1)) : na, title = 'level 1', color = color0, display = display.price_scale)
plot(array.size(Dividedstrings) >= 4 ? str.tonumber(array.get(Dividedstrings, 3)) : na, title = 'level 2', color = color1, display = display.price_scale)
plot(array.size(Dividedstrings) >= 6 ? str.tonumber(array.get(Dividedstrings, 5)) : na, title = 'level 3', color = color2, display = display.price_scale)

    for i = 0 to array.size(Dividedstrings) - 1 by 1

                array.push(symbolThresholds, line)

    result = ''

    for i = 0 to array.size(symbolThresholds) - 1 by 2

                         i <= 5 ? color2 :
                         i <= 7 ? color3 :
                         i <= 9 ? color4 :
                         i <= 11 ? color5 :
                         i <= 13 ? color6 :
                         i <= 15 ? color7 :
                         i <= 17 ? color8 :
                         color9


        if i % 2 == 0 and array.size(Dividedstrings) > i + 1
            label.new(bar_index, str.tonumber(array.get(Dividedstrings, i + 1)), array.get(Dividedstrings, i),
                      xloc = xloc.bar_index, color = color.new(color.white, 10),
                      size = size.large, style = label.style_none,
                      textcolor = temporarycolor, textalign = text.align_right)


        if i % 2 != 0 and array.size(Dividedstrings) > i
            line.new(bar_index, str.tonumber(array.get(Dividedstrings, i)),
                     bar_index + 1, str.tonumber(array.get(Dividedstrings, i)),
                     extend = extend.both, color = temporarycolor)
comment.raw_body:
//@version=6
indicator('Mean Reversion Threshold Levels', overlay = true)


// Input for multiple symbols with their respective threshold levels
input_text = input.text_area('', title = 'Input Symbol Thresholds (Format: SYMBOL on one line, thresholds on next line)', confirm = true)


// Get current symbol
current_symbol = syminfo.ticker


// Color inputs for different levels
color0 = input.color(defval = color.rgb(224, 10, 110), title = 'Color 1')
color1 = input.color(defval = color.rgb(224, 10, 110), title = 'Color 2')
color2 = input.color(defval = color.rgb(224, 10, 110), title = 'Color 3')
color3 = input.color(defval = color.rgb(224, 10, 110), title = 'Color 4')
color4 = input.color(defval = color.rgb(224, 10, 110), title = 'Color 5')
color5 = input.color(defval = color.rgb(224, 10, 110), title = 'Color 6')
color6 = input.color(defval = color.rgb(224, 10, 110), title = 'Color 7')
color7 = input.color(defval = color.rgb(224, 10, 110), title = 'Color 8')
color8 = input.color(defval = color.rgb(224, 10, 110), title = 'Color 9')
color9 = input.color(defval = color.rgb(224, 10, 110), title = 'Color 10')


// Function to split string by a delimiter
TxtToArray(string origin, string divider) =>
    str.split(origin, divider)


// Updated parsing logic supporting old and new formats
ParseSymbolThresholds(string inputText) =>
    symbolThresholds = array.new_string()
    if str.length(inputText) > 0
        lines = TxtToArray(inputText, '\n')
        currentSymbol = ''
        for i = 0 to array.size(lines) - 1
            line = str.trim(array.get(lines, i))


            // New format: === SYMBOL ===
            if str.startswith(line, '===') and str.endswith(line, '===') and str.length(line) > 7
                words = TxtToArray(line," ")
                symbolName = array.get(words,1)
                currentSymbol := symbolName
            // Old format: line without commas and not empty
            else if not str.contains(line, ',') and str.length(line) > 0
                currentSymbol := str.trim(line)
            // If this line is data and currentSymbol is set
            else if str.contains(line, ',') and str.length(currentSymbol) > 0
                array.push(symbolThresholds, currentSymbol)
                array.push(symbolThresholds, line)
                currentSymbol := ''
    symbolThresholds


// Normalize and match against current symbol
GetCurrentSymbolThresholds(array<string> symbolThresholds, string currentSymbol) =>
    result = ''
    normalizedCurrent = str.upper(currentSymbol)
    for i = 0 to array.size(symbolThresholds) - 1 by 2
        if i + 1 < array.size(symbolThresholds)
            symbol = array.get(symbolThresholds, i)
            thresholds = array.get(symbolThresholds, i + 1)


            normalizedInput = str.upper(symbol)
            normalizedInput := str.replace(normalizedInput, '=F', '1!')
            normalizedInput := normalizedInput == 'ES' ? 'ES1!' : normalizedInput


            if normalizedInput == normalizedCurrent
                result := thresholds
                break
    result


// Parse input and get current symbol thresholds
symbolThresholds = ParseSymbolThresholds(input_text)
currentThresholds = GetCurrentSymbolThresholds(symbolThresholds, current_symbol)
Dividedstrings = TxtToArray(currentThresholds, ',')


// Plot levels
plot(array.size(Dividedstrings) >= 2 ? str.tonumber(array.get(Dividedstrings, 1)) : na, title = 'level 1', color = color0, display = display.price_scale)
plot(array.size(Dividedstrings) >= 4 ? str.tonumber(array.get(Dividedstrings, 3)) : na, title = 'level 2', color = color1, display = display.price_scale)
plot(array.size(Dividedstrings) >= 6 ? str.tonumber(array.get(Dividedstrings, 5)) : na, title = 'level 3', color = color2, display = display.price_scale)
plot(array.size(Dividedstrings) >= 8 ? str.tonumber(array.get(Dividedstrings, 7)) : na, title = 'level 4', color = color3, display = display.price_scale)
plot(array.size(Dividedstrings) >= 10 ? str.tonumber(array.get(Dividedstrings, 9)) : na, title = 'level 5', color = color4, display = display.price_scale)
plot(array.size(Dividedstrings) >= 12 ? str.tonumber(array.get(Dividedstrings, 11)) : na, title = 'level 6', color = color5, display = display.price_scale)
plot(array.size(Dividedstrings) >= 14 ? str.tonumber(array.get(Dividedstrings, 13)) : na, title = 'level 7', color = color6, display = display.price_scale)
plot(array.size(Dividedstrings) >= 16 ? str.tonumber(array.get(Dividedstrings, 15)) : na, title = 'level 8', color = color7, display = display.price_scale)
plot(array.size(Dividedstrings) >= 18 ? str.tonumber(array.get(Dividedstrings, 17)) : na, title = 'level 9', color = color8, display = display.price_scale)
plot(array.size(Dividedstrings) >= 20 ? str.tonumber(array.get(Dividedstrings, 19)) : na, title = 'level 10', color = color9, display = display.price_scale)


// Add labels and lines
if barstate.islastconfirmedhistory and array.size(Dividedstrings) != 0
    for i = 0 to array.size(Dividedstrings) - 1 by 1
        temporarycolor = i <= 1 ? color0 :
                         i <= 3 ? color1 :
                         i <= 5 ? color2 :
                         i <= 7 ? color3 :
                         i <= 9 ? color4 :
                         i <= 11 ? color5 :
                         i <= 13 ? color6 :
                         i <= 15 ? color7 :
                         i <= 17 ? color8 :
                         color9


        if i % 2 == 0 and array.size(Dividedstrings) > i + 1
            label.new(bar_index, str.tonumber(array.get(Dividedstrings, i + 1)), array.get(Dividedstrings, i),
                      xloc = xloc.bar_index, color = color.new(color.white, 10),
                      size = size.large, style = label.style_none,
                      textcolor = temporarycolor, textalign = text.align_right)


        if i % 2 != 0 and array.size(Dividedstrings) > i
            line.new(bar_index, str.tonumber(array.get(Dividedstrings, i)),
                     bar_index + 1, str.tonumber(array.get(Dividedstrings, i)),
                     extend = extend.both, color = temporarycolor)
